0.2.5.1.description:
### What's New?
- Added new location checks based on in-game settings, expanding the 
  number of locations available in your randomizer seed by a maximum of 60!
- Added logic surrounding setting checks, ensuring items and locations 
  are reachable in a valid and fair way
- Added options to configure which setting checks are included in your game

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed the manifest having incorrect version info, which may have caused 
  issues with Archipelago recognizing the correct apworld version

### New YAML Options

**`setting_checks`** — Enables location checks for Country, City, and 
Alpine settings (cosmetic variants). Set your preferred option to `50` 
and the other to `0`.

**`setting_check_mode`** — Controls which settings have checks:
- `base_only` *(default)* — Only base settings have checks
- `base_and_extras` — Base settings + any extras you define
- `extras_only` — Only the extras you define

**`setting_extra_checks`** — A list of extra settings to add checks for.
Example: `[autumn, witch, turbo]` — generates 15 day checks for each.
- Must be **lowercase**
- Only active when `setting_check_mode` is `base_and_extras` or `extras_only`

> ⚠️ I'd recommend experimenting before using these in an actual multiworld 
> seed — they can be tricky to configure!


### Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you 
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 0.2.5.1](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/0.2.5.1)
0.2.5.1.title: PlateUpAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.1

0.2.5.2.description:
### What's New?

#### New Goal: Reach Day X with Dishes
A new goal has been added where you must reach a target day while
completing runs with specific dishes.

#### Gated Appliances
Appliances are now gated behind unlock items! They will no longer 
appear in the shop until received from the multiworld. You always 
start with the essentials needed for your starting dish.

#### New Items
- **Shop Size Increase** — Expands the number of appliances available 
  in the shop each day
- **Coin Bundles** — New filler items that give you a coin boost

#### New Starting Cards
Configure how many difficult cards you start with using the new options.

### New YAML Options

**`day_target`** — The day you must reach to complete the new goal

**`dish_goal_count`** — How many dishes must be completed for the new goal

**`starting_cards`** — Enable/disable starting with difficult cards

**`starting_cards_amount`** — How many difficult cards to start with

**`appliance_gating`** — Enable/disable appliance gating entirely

### Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 2.5.2](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/0.2.5.2)
0.2.5.2.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.2

0.2.5.3.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.3

0.2.5.5.description:
## What's New?
- Added a bunch of missing appliance unlock items that weren't showing up before
- Added new filler items: Patience Increase, Less Customers, Minimum Group Size Decrease, Maximum Group Size Decrease, and Mess Reduction
- Added new traps: Patience Decrease, More Customers, Minimum Group Size Increase, and Maximum Group Size Increase
- Added two new progression items — Reduce Group Size and Global Patience Increase — tied to new unlock gates
- Added achievement checks for most in-game achievements (dish-specific ones are excluded for now)

## New YAML Options
**`free_starter_dishes`** — Controls how many dishes you start with already unlocked when playing with locked dishes. Defaults to 1 (the classic single free starter). Set to 0 to unlock everything from scratch, or go higher to begin with more dishes ready to go.

**`day_leases_enabled`** — Toggles Day Lease progression items on or off. When off, no lease items are placed and days are never gated by leases.

**`starting_group_size`** — Sets the starting customer group size for your run (0–8). Higher values make things a lot harder. When enabled, it places Reduce Group Size progression items spread evenly across the run. Set to 0 to leave it vanilla.

**`global_patience_enabled`** — Turns on Global Patience Increase progression items. When on, a set number of patience upgrades are required to reach later days, spread evenly across the run.

**`global_patience_upgrade_count`** — How many Global Patience Increase items get placed (1–10). Only matters when `global_patience_enabled` is turned on.

**`achievement_checks`** — Enables in-game achievements as location checks. Some are tied to specific goals or appliance unlocks, so keep that in mind when building your yaml.

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 2.5.5](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/0.2.5.5)
0.2.5.5.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.5

0.2.5.6.description:
## What's New?
- Added a toggleable money cap — when on, you can't hold more gold than your current cap allows until you find Money Cap Increase items
- Added a new option to control how much gold each Money Cap Increase item adds
- Added an option to choose whether finding an appliance unlock immediately hands you that appliance, or just adds it to future shops
- Added new reroll cost checks tied to your money cap settings
- Dining Table is now guaranteed to show up early so you're never stuck without one
- Added several missing appliance unlock items that were blocking progression

## New YAML Options
**`money_cap_enabled`** — Turns on a gold cap mechanic. While active, you can't hold more than your current maximum, which only goes up when you find Money Cap Increase items in the multiworld.

**`starting_money_cap`** — Sets your starting gold cap (10–40). You won't be able to hold more than this until you find increases.

**`money_cap_increase_amount`** — How much gold each Money Cap Increase item adds to your cap (5–100).

**`appliance_unlock_grants_appliance`** — When on, finding an appliance unlock item immediately gives you that appliance to use. When off, it only adds it to future shop rotations.

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 0.2.5.6](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/0.2.5.6)
0.2.5.6.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.6

0.2.5.7.description:
## What's New?
- Money cap now has a guaranteed minimum ceiling so you can actually afford appliances — cap increases will always get you to at least 60 gold max
- Patience and Mess Reduction upgrades show up less often and are spread out more across the run
- More Customers and Group Size Increase traps appear more frequently
- When playing with a starting group size, the Reduce Group Size items now come later in the run so the early game difficulty actually sticks around for longer

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!

0.2.5.7r2.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.7

0.2.5.8.description:
## What's New?
- Fixed the game name capitalization to "PlateUp" (it's the little things)
- Added `trap_chance` — control exactly what percentage of your filler slots become traps
- Added filler percentage options for Patience Increase, Less Customers, Group Size Decrease, and Mess Reduction — tune your filler pool to your liking
- Added `money_cap_activation` — choose whether your gold cap updates instantly or waits until the start of the next day
- Added `global_patience_starting_debuff` — set how much patience is knocked off at the start of your run (it'll always recover to normal with all upgrades)
- Clarified and cleaned up YAML option descriptions across the board
- The Archipelago options page is now fully supported — you can build your YAML there instead of editing the file manually!

## New YAML Options
**`trap_chance`** — Sets what percentage of your remaining filler slots turn into traps. At 0 it falls back to the old auto-scaling behavior; at 100 it's wall-to-wall chaos

**`patience_filler_percent`** — How much of your filler pool to fill with Patience Increase items, giving you temporary customer patience buffs throughout the run

**`customer_filler_percent`** — How much of your filler pool to fill with Less Customers items, occasionally giving you a lighter service day

**`group_size_filler_percent`** — How much of your filler pool to fill with Group Size Decrease items, reducing how many people show up per table

**`mess_reduction_percent`** — How much of your filler pool to fill with Mess Reduction items, cutting down on cleanup burden during a day

**`money_cap_activation`** — Controls when a gold cap increase kicks in: either the moment you receive it, or at the start of your next cooking day

**`global_patience_starting_debuff`** — How much patience is shaved off at the very start of your run. Recovers to normal as you collect Global Patience Increase items

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 0.2.5.8](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/0.2.5.8)
0.2.5.8.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.8

0.2.5.9.description:
## What's New?
- Achievement checks are now configurable — choose between all checks, a filtered set that excludes Charcoal Factory and Safety Last when appliance unlocks are off, or none at all
- Five new traps added to the pool: Random Dish Extra, Random Side Dish, Tip Jar Drain, Good Advertisement, and Card Swap
- You can now control how many appliance unlock items appear in the pool (10–93), giving you flexibility over how much progression gating you want
- Appliances without an unlock item in the pool can now be toggled on or off in the shop
- Cleaned up WebWorld so the options page looks a bit cleaner

## New YAML Options

**`trap_weights`** — Set the weight for each individual trap, letting you control which ones show up and how often. Set a trap's weight to 0 to remove it from the pool entirely.

**`money_cap_increase_count`** — Controls how many Money Cap Increase items the generator places into the item pool.

**`coin_filler_percent`** — Sets what percentage of your filler items will be coin pickups.

**`achievement_check_mode`** — Decides which achievement checks appear in your pool. `all` includes everything, `earned_only` filters out Charcoal Factory and Safety Last when appliance unlocks are disabled, and `none` removes achievement checks entirely.

**`appliance_unlock_pool_size`** — Controls how many appliance unlock items are placed in the pool, from 10 up to 93. Useful for making sure all the appliances you need for progression can actually show up.

**`unlocked_appliances_in_shop`** — Toggles whether appliances that don't have an unlock item in the pool are still available to buy in the shop.

**`extra_starting_cards`** — Adds extra cards to your starting hand using the same rules as difficulty cards — they carry over between runs and are removed via the Remove Card mechanic.

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 2.5.9](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/2.5.9)
0.2.5.9.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.5-beta.9

0.2.6.0.description:
## What's New?
- Blueprint Check Pedestals are now in the game! Three pedestals spawn on the street outside your restaurant, each showing a check you can buy for a set price. The price and number of checks available are fully configurable in your YAML.
- Item scouting is now done on connect, so each pedestal shows the actual name of the item it'll send, including the player it belongs to if it's for someone else in the multiworld.
- Improved error messaging when your YAML settings result in too few locations for the item pool.
- Default option values have been adjusted to better suit the integration out of the box.

## New YAML Options
**`blueprint_check_count`** — How many blueprint checks to add to the pool. Each one spawns a purchasable pedestal check over the course of your run.

**`blueprint_base_price`** — The starting price for buying a blueprint check.

**`blueprint_price_increase`** — How much the price goes up after each blueprint check is purchased.

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you update both together for everything to work correctly!
[Mod Update 0.2.6.0](https://github.com/CazIsABoi/PlateUpAPMod/releases/tag/)
0.2.6.0.title: PlateupAP apworld v0.2.6-beta.0

0.2.6.1.description:
## What's New?
- `trap_cards` has been removed — set `trap_chance` to 0 to disable traps instead
- Dish-specific day leases are here — each dish now has its own lease items that
  only gate progress on that dish specifically, leaving other dishes freely accessible
- When using dish-specific leases with the franchise or complete days goals, overflow
  days beyond what dish leases cover are gated by a new Overtime Day Lease item
- Added `dish_lease_scope` for the reach day with dishes goal — choose whether all
  selected dishes are lease-gated, or only the first few needed to hit your dish goal.
  With this goal and dish-specific mode, no generic leases are generated at all
- Blueprint and setting checks now generate correctly for all goals, not just the first two
- "Lose a Run" is now a valid check for franchise and complete days goals
- Fixed a bug where goal state could bleed between games and cause generation failures

## New YAML Options
**`day_lease_mode`** — Controls how Day Lease items are organised. `global` uses a
single shared lease item that gates all day-based content as before. `dish_specific`
gives each dish its own named lease items (e.g. Steak Day Lease) that only gate that
dish's days — requires dish count to be greater than 0.

**`dish_lease_scope`** — When using dish-specific leases with the reach day with dishes
goal, controls which dishes get lease items. `all_dishes` gates every selected dish.
`goal_count_only` only gates the first dish_goal_count dishes, leaving the rest freely
accessible. Only relevant when day_lease_mode is dish_specific.

## Note
This apworld update is directly tied to the mod update — make sure you
update both together for everything to work correctly!
